scope: Worked example for Wymark + Swift Current only; no direct mutation of quarters-data.js or data.js.

anchor_truth_rule.csv_titled_to_mfl: confirmed MFL-titled at 2026-01-18 snapshot; add/keep/reassign under correct property id
anchor_truth_rule.current_not_in_csv: keep on map and flag; never delete from this pass
anchor_truth_rule.csv_points_to_other_property: reassign by property id, preserving parcel intel and recomputing geometry when granularity changes

granularity_surprises: Swift Current has 31 CSV title rows but not 31 clean prebuilt polygons: 14 ADD title rows, 17 REASSIGN title rows, and only 16 unique current Wymark source features because NW-7-13-14-W3 has two title rows for one mapped location.

Wymark current map has 101 unique legal-location features from 131 Wymark.xlsx title rows; final application should preserve the one-feature-per-legal-location runtime pattern unless the renderer is deliberately changed.

Several Swift Current CSV rows are LSD-level while current Wymark source records are quarter-level or partial-quarter sale-package records; geometry needs pipeline computation, not copy/paste of legacy polygons.

_other_affected.note: Only Wymark and Swift Current are touched by this worked example. No other property bucket is changed by these decisions.

decisions_log.decision: Do not delete any Wymark current map feature that is absent from the MFL CSV.
decisions_log.rationale: The CSV is title-owner anchor truth for MONETTE FARMS LTD. only, not the whole operating footprint. Absence can mean sale, lease/rent-back, different Monette entity, personal/related ownership, or missing title export coverage.

decisions_log.decision: Split Wymark audit FLAGs into 16 unique reassign_out records and 66 true follow-up flags.
decisions_log.rationale: The Wymark audit report is incoming-only; Swift Current audit shows 17 CSV title rows currently filed under Wymark, collapsing to 16 unique current map features because NW-7-13-14-W3 has two title rows.

decisions_log.decision: Keep 19 unique Wymark map features as MFL-titled verified, with 22 CSV title matches attached.
decisions_log.rationale: The CSV confirms the Wymark Lac Pelletier T11/R13-R14 core. Duplicate title rows share some current map features, so final application should mark the feature verified while preserving all title references.

decisions_log.decision: Move the Swift Current CSV-confirmed Wymark source features to swift-current, but recompute geometry before final apply where CSV granularity is LSD-level.
decisions_log.rationale: Parcel ownership/property-id truth comes from the CSV crosswalk, while the existing Wymark source geometry may be quarter-level or aggregated sale-package geometry. Copying it blindly would overstate some title footprints.

decisions_log.decision: Treat the five Carefoot/Waldeck quarters as flagged knowledge-layer parcels, not MFL-title-confirmed parcels.
decisions_log.rationale: They remain important public intel and map context, but they are absent from the 2026-01-18 MFL CSV and the court-affidavit acreage does not match the five-quarter title total.

decisions_log.decision: Treat Section 18-12-14-W3 feedlot-proposal parcels as follow-up flags, despite old local/title-package intel saying MFL ownership.
decisions_log.rationale: They are absent from the MFL CSV snapshot, so public copy should not claim MFL title until fresh ISC title pulls reconcile corporate/personal/other-entity ownership.

decisions_log.decision: Populate Swift Current geometry from CSV-confirmed RM Swift Current 137 rows, not from the old point-only data.js record.
decisions_log.rationale: The data.js Swift Current record is a known point-only court-file placeholder. The CSV provides concrete parcel title rows for T13/R13-R14/W3, including Stewart Valley-area parcels.

decisions_log.decision: Do not duplicate same-legal-location rows as overlapping map polygons without an explicit aggregation plan.
decisions_log.rationale: The existing pipeline has already collapsed 131 Wymark.xlsx title rows into 101 Wymark map features. CSV duplicate legal locations need title metadata aggregation or a renderer-level title list, not accidental double painting.
evidence: Wymark.xlsx sheet 1-Wymark total 131 data rows / 101 unique locs
